# Ground Truth: Vataxia Social Network API

## Metadata
- **Date audited:** 2026-03-16
- **Audited by:** Auto-generated auditor
- **Skill focus:** Data mapping and inventory - identifying all personal data collection, storage, flows, third-party sharing, and retention practices

## Must-Find Items

Critical findings any competent assessment should identify within this skill's focus area.

| # | Finding | Category | Expected Severity | Expected Confidence | Reasoning | Acceptable Alternatives |
|---|---------|----------|-------------------|---------------------|-----------|------------------------|
| 1 | User email addresses stored in `accounts.User.email` field (v1/accounts/models/user.py:9) as unique identifier and authentication credential | Direct Identifier PII | HIGH | HIGH | Email is the USERNAME_FIELD for authentication, stored in PostgreSQL (production) or SQLite (dev/local), used for login and user identification | May classify as "authentication credential" or "contact information" |
| 2 | User first and last names stored in `accounts.User.first_name` and `accounts.User.last_name` fields (v1/accounts/models/user.py:10-11) | Direct Identifier PII | MEDIUM | HIGH | Real names collected at registration (POST /users, /accept_invitation endpoints), no apparent business justification for requiring real names in social network | Could classify as "profile data" or "personal identifiers" |
| 3 | User profile images stored via `accounts.Profile.image` ImageField (v1/accounts/models/profile.py:6), uploaded to media directory or S3 in production | Biometric/Image PII | MEDIUM | HIGH | Profile pictures could contain facial images (biometric data under GDPR), stored locally in /media or AWS S3 via django-storages (aws_storage_classes.py), no retention policy defined | May note uncertain S3 configuration (aws_storage_classes.py exists but no AWS keys in settings) |
| 4 | Private message content stored in `private_messages.PrivateMessage.subject` and `private_messages.PrivateMessage.body` fields (v1/private_messages/models/private_message.py:9-10) | Communication Content | HIGH | HIGH | End-to-end private communications between users, no encryption mentioned, stored in plaintext in database, could contain sensitive personal disclosures | N/A |
| 5 | Financial transaction data in `credits.Transfer` model tracking sender, receiver, and amount (v1/credits/models/transfer.py:7-9) | Financial Data | HIGH | HIGH | Credit transfer system creates permanent audit trail of financial transactions between users, includes sender/receiver user relationships and amounts | May be characterized as "virtual currency transactions" or "in-app purchase data" |
| 6 | Post and reply content in `posts.Post.body` and `replies.PostReply.body` fields (v1/posts/models/post.py:9, v1/replies/models/reply.py:8) | User Generated Content | MEDIUM | HIGH | Public user-generated content that could contain personal information, opinions, or sensitive disclosures | N/A |
| 7 | User voting behavior tracked in `votes.PostVote` model linking users to posts with vote values (v1/votes/models/post_vote.py:6-8) | Behavioral Data | MEDIUM | HIGH | Tracks user preferences and opinions through voting (upvote/downvote), reveals political/ideological leanings, unique constraint on (post, user) | N/A |
| 8 | Password reset codes stored in `accounts.ResetPasswordCode` model with UUID codes linked to users (v1/accounts/models/reset_password_code.py:6-8) | Authentication Data | HIGH | HIGH | Sensitive authentication tokens stored indefinitely (no expiration), deleted only after use (v1/accounts/views/reset_password.py:31), creates security risk if database compromised | Could note "no expiration timestamp" as separate finding |
| 9 | Authentication tokens stored via Django REST Framework's authtoken.Token (config/settings/base.py:30, referenced in v1/accounts/fixtures/token.json) | Authentication Data | HIGH | HIGH | API tokens for session management, no rotation policy visible, stored permanently until logout (v1/accounts/views/logout.py:19) | N/A |
| 10 | AWS RDS PostgreSQL database in production stores all user data at xxx.xxx.xxx.rds.amazonaws.com (config/settings/production.py:16) | Data Storage Location | HIGH | MEDIUM | Production database hosted on AWS RDS (cross-border transfer if users outside AWS region), credentials visible in code (placeholder 'xxx'), SQLite for dev/local | May flag as LOW confidence if AWS region unknown, making cross-border determination uncertain |
| 11 | User account metadata: `date_joined`, `last_login`, `is_active`, `is_staff`, `is_superuser` fields (v1/accounts/models/user.py:8,12-13) inherited from AbstractBaseUser and PermissionsMixin | Account Metadata | MEDIUM | HIGH | Tracks account creation timestamp, last authentication time, and privilege escalation; `last_login` updated automatically by Django on successful authentication | N/A |
| 12 | Invitation tracking system in `credits.Invitation` model linking sender to receiver with unique UUID codes (v1/credits/models/invitation.py:8-10) | Relationship Data | MEDIUM | HIGH | Tracks user referral relationships and social graph, receiver populated when invitation accepted (v1/accounts/serializers/accept_invitation.py:26) | N/A |
| 13 | Django sessions stored via django.contrib.sessions middleware (config/settings/base.py:40), session backend not explicitly configured (defaults to database) | Session Data | MEDIUM | MEDIUM | Session data likely stored in database (default), could contain authenticated user state, no explicit session timeout configured | Could be HIGH confidence if assessor checks Django defaults, MEDIUM if uncertain about session storage location |
| 14 | No data retention policy or automated deletion mechanism found in codebase | Data Retention Gap | HIGH | HIGH | User data, posts, messages, votes, and transactions persist indefinitely with no cleanup jobs, violates storage minimization principle | N/A |
| 15 | Manual user deletion available via DELETE /users/{user_id} endpoint (v1/accounts/views/user.py:68-87) but no cascade deletion policy defined on foreign keys | Deletion Mechanism | MEDIUM | HIGH | Users can be deleted by moderators/admins, but Django models lack explicit on_delete parameters (defaults to CASCADE), so related data fate is uncertain without migration inspection | May classify as "incomplete deletion mechanism" |

## Nice-to-Find Items

Deeper findings that demonstrate thoroughness.

| # | Finding | Category | Expected Severity | Expected Confidence | Reasoning |
|---|---------|----------|-------------------|---------------------|-----------|
| 1 | Moderator sponsorship relationships tracked in `user_roles.Moderator.sponsor` field (v1/user_roles/models/moderator.py:7) | Relationship Data | LOW | HIGH | Creates hierarchical relationship graph showing which administrator promoted which moderator, could be used for trust/reputation analysis |
| 2 | Post and profile images uploaded by users without visible file type validation beyond Django's ImageField (v1/posts/models/post.py:10, v1/accounts/models/profile.py:6) | Data Collection Pattern | LOW | MEDIUM | Image uploads could include EXIF metadata containing GPS coordinates, camera info, timestamps - no apparent EXIF stripping |
| 3 | User filtering available via query parameters exposing user IDs (v1/filters/posts/post.py, v1/filters/credits/transfer.py) | Data Exposure | LOW | HIGH | API endpoints allow filtering posts by user ID, transfers by sender/receiver ID, enabling profiling of specific users' activity |
| 4 | CreatedModified mixin adds `created_date` and `modified_date` timestamps to Posts, PrivateMessages, Transfers, Invitations, Administrators, Moderators (v1/general/created_modified.py:4-6) | Temporal Data | LOW | HIGH | Auto_now_add and auto_now fields track creation and modification times for user activities, enabling temporal behavior analysis |
| 5 | CORS configured to allow all origins (CORS_ORIGIN_ALLOW_ALL = True in config/settings/base.py:94) | Data Sharing Pattern | MEDIUM | HIGH | Permits any website to make authenticated API requests from user browsers, could enable third-party sites to access user data with stolen tokens |
| 6 | Debug toolbar installed in development (requirements/base.txt:4, config/settings/development.py:17) | Development Tool Risk | LOW | MEDIUM | Django Debug Toolbar could expose sensitive data if accidentally enabled in production, though currently only in dev settings |
| 7 | Hardcoded SECRET_KEY in base settings (config/settings/base.py:5) | Security Configuration | MEDIUM | HIGH | Django SECRET_KEY 'a1y425b-u7(1_j%ekoh+_v_d620p5l5zfl6+fq&vwd+suq^j*z' is hardcoded and likely shared across environments, used for cryptographic signing of sessions/cookies |
| 8 | User wallet balance tracking in `credits.Wallet.balance` field (v1/credits/models/wallet.py:6) | Financial Data | MEDIUM | HIGH | Virtual currency balance per user, automatically created on first access (v1/credits/views/wallet.py:19), modified through transfers and invitations |
| 9 | AWS S3 storage classes defined for static and media files (aws_storage_classes.py:4-9) but no AWS credentials configured in visible settings | Third-Party Processor | MEDIUM | LOW | django-storages and boto libraries installed (requirements/production.txt:4-7) suggesting S3 intent, but no AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY/AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME in settings files |
| 10 | Sample fixture data includes real-looking email addresses and names (admin@email.com, moderator@email.com, normal@email.com, "Bucky Roberts", "Matt Smith", "Norma Jean" in v1/accounts/fixtures/user.json) | Test Data | LOW | MEDIUM | Fixture data could be loaded to production if script run incorrectly, contains potentially real person names |
| 11 | uWSGI REMOTE_ADDR parameter configured (uwsgi_params:13) to pass IP addresses from web server | Network Data | MEDIUM | MEDIUM | IP addresses accessible to application via request.META, though not visibly stored in models, could be logged or processed at runtime |
| 12 | No visible encryption for passwords beyond Django defaults (AbstractBaseUser provides password hashing) | Data Protection | LOW | HIGH | Relies on Django's PBKDF2 password hashing (visible in fixtures: pbkdf2_sha256$36000$...), password validators configured (config/settings/base.py:52-57) |
| 13 | All user data accessible to administrators via Django admin interface (v1/accounts/admin.py:11-13) | Access Pattern | MEDIUM | HIGH | Admin users have unrestricted access to all User, Profile, ResetPasswordCode data through Django admin panel at /admin/ |
| 14 | Default permission class IsAuthenticatedOrReadOnly (config/settings/base.py:61) allows unauthenticated users to read posts, user profiles, and votes | Data Exposure | MEDIUM | HIGH | Public read access to user names, emails, profile pictures, posts, and voting behavior without authentication |

## Known Ambiguities

Findings where reasonable reviewers might disagree.

| Finding | Why It's Ambiguous | Acceptable Interpretations |
|---------|-------------------|---------------------------|
| AWS S3 usage for media storage | aws_storage_classes.py and boto libraries present but no AWS credentials configured in settings files, production.py uses local MEDIA_ROOT | (A) S3 is configured via environment variables not visible in code - should list as third-party processor with LOW confidence, OR (B) S3 classes are legacy/unused - should note as potential third-party sharing but mark as not currently active |
| Email address sensitivity classification | Email used as username/identifier vs. contact information | (A) Classify as "authentication credential" emphasizing security aspect, OR (B) Classify as "contact information" emphasizing communication aspect, OR (C) Classify as "direct identifier" emphasizing PII aspect - all valid |
| Cross-border data transfer determination | Production database at xxx.rds.amazonaws.com but AWS region unknown | (A) Mark as "cross-border transfer: unknown" with MEDIUM confidence, OR (B) Mark as "data transfer to AWS processor" without cross-border determination (LOW confidence), OR (C) Note as potential cross-border transfer requiring investigation |
| Session data storage and contents | Sessions middleware enabled but SESSION_ENGINE not explicitly configured | (A) Assume Django default (database-backed sessions) and list session data in database, OR (B) Mark session storage as "undefined/requires investigation" (MEDIUM confidence), OR (C) Investigate settings.py imports for session configuration elsewhere |
| Legal basis for processing | No explicit consent mechanism, terms of service, or privacy policy visible in code | (A) Mark all fields as "Legal Basis: TBD" requiring legal review, OR (B) Infer "Contract" basis for core functionality fields and "Legitimate Interest" for optional fields, OR (C) Note as "undetermined - no visible consent mechanism" |
| Profile images as biometric data | Could contain faces but purpose is user identification not biometric authentication | (A) Classify as "biometric data" under GDPR Article 9, OR (B) Classify as "image data" not specifically biometric since not used for authentication, OR (C) Note as "potential biometric data depending on usage" |
| Wallet balance as financial data vs. virtual currency | In-app credits may not represent real money | (A) Classify as "financial data" if credits can be purchased or cashed out, OR (B) Classify as "virtual currency/game credits" if purely in-app, OR (C) Mark as "requires business context to classify" |

## Red Herrings

Things that look like issues within this skill's focus area but are not.

| Item | Why It Looks Like an Issue | Why It's Not |
|------|--------------------------|-------------|
| SECRET_KEY in base.py | Looks like credentials are exposed in code | This is a configuration issue, not personal data collection/storage. While security-relevant, it's outside the scope of data inventory mapping |
| DEBUG = True in development.py | Looks like production security issue | This is development configuration only (config/environment.py shows ENVIRONMENT toggle), not active in production settings where DEBUG = False |
| Hardcoded database password 'xxx' in production.py | Looks like exposed credentials | These are placeholder values meant to be replaced, not actual credentials. Not personal data - this is a configuration/security issue |
| ALLOWED_HOSTS = ['*'] | Looks like security vulnerability | This is a security configuration issue, not personal data handling. Doesn't affect what data is collected or stored |
| Password field in User model (v1/accounts/models/user.py) | Looks like passwords stored in plaintext | Django AbstractBaseUser provides automatic password hashing. Fixtures show hashed passwords (pbkdf2_sha256$...). This is properly protected credential storage, not a finding |
| 'groups' and 'user_permissions' fields in User fixture | Look like additional personal data fields | These are Django's built-in permission system fields (from PermissionsMixin), used for authorization logic, not personal data collection |
| User ID integers throughout the codebase | Look like they might be sensitive identifiers | Sequential integer primary keys are technical database identifiers, not PII on their own. Only relevant when combined with other data in relationships |
| request.data.get() calls in views | Look like data collection points | These are API request handlers - the actual storage points are the model definitions. Views are the mechanism but models are the source of truth for what's stored |
| dump_database.py script | Looks like data export mechanism for DSAR | This script removes migrations and database files (scripts/dump_database.py:19-24), it's a development cleanup tool, not a data export feature |
